mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159398 thousand as of May 2013 reflects post-recession labor recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5% annualized growth, the real policy rate becomes restrictive. This threshold-triggered condition signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 319.615 as of May 2013
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with real rate constraint
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect (joint condition) shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals deteriorating residential investment demand and forward-looking weakness in construction employment and consumer wealth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the demand shock propagates through the economy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1501.0 thousand units as of April 2013
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of cases, or the lagged correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent 60-day returns is not statistically significant (|r| < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains below 4.5%, indicating supply-side capacity utilization is rising without slack. This interaction-driven mechanism signals inflationary pressure and Fed tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.2062 and UNRATE at 4.2% as of April 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient in regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) relative to additive single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling weakening consumer demand and forward earnings pressure on equities. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 722708.0 million dollars as of April 2013
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration below 0.2% fails to produce negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or rolling 1-year Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month while payroll growth (PAYEMS) simultaneously declines, signaling a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice recession risk and earnings forecasts downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE month-over-month increase > 0.3pp AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159398 thousand as of May 2013
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in a logistic model controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, creating a regime where monetary policy is restrictive relative to growth. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the Fed faces pressure to ease and equity valuations compress under slowing earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23542.349 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2013, FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of May 2013
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing growth
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the lagged correlation between the joint condition and subsequent 60-day returns is not statistically significant (|r| < 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues to rise, signaling stagflationary pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings under cost pressures and demand destruction, and the Fed faces conflicting policy objectives.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.2062 and CPIAUCSL at 319.615 as of April-May 2013
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or interaction term in regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) relative to additive single-series specification.
